zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Creye於1242年出生在英國林可爾史爾可比時，她的父親Simon Creye27歲，母親Agnes27歲。她與Gilbert de Pecche結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1302年在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾咸頓 在 阿爾登享年60歲，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾咸頓 在 阿爾登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Creye于1242年出生在英国林可爾史爾可比时，她的父亲Simon Creye27岁，母亲Agnes27岁。她与Gilbert de Pecche结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1302年在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾咸頓 在 阿爾登享年60岁，埋葬在英国瓦維其可爾咸頓 在 阿爾登。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Creye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adrien, Alain, Camille, Fernand, Gaetan, Gilles, Gillis, Martial, Michel, Monique, Normand, Rosaire.
nickname for an overproud man, from Old French creste ‘crest (of a hill)’ (from Late Latin crista), used with reference to the comb of a rooster.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Creye was born in 1242, in Corby, Lincolnshire, England, her father, Simon Creye, was 27 and her mother, Agnes, was 27. She married Gilbert de Pecche. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1302, in Hampton in Arden, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 60, and was buried in Hampton in Arden, Warwickshire, England.

Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1302, à Hampton in Arden, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterrée à Hampton in Arden, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Creye(이)가 1242년 영국 링코실르 코르비에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Simon Creye(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 Gilbert de Pecche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1302년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 함프턴 인 아든에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르 함프턴 인 아든에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Creye родилась в 1242 г. в местности корби, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Simon Creye было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gilbert de Pecche. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1302 г. в местности хамптон ин арден, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 60 лет и была похоронена в местности хамптон ин арден, варвиккшир, Англия.
